key_insight: MinSurf turns mineral surface modeling from choosing a few common facets into a high-throughput, atomistically resolved search over surface terminations, energies, and equilibrium morphologies.
engineering_relevance: This enables more physically representative interface models for applications such as carbon mineralization, geo-energy storage, contaminant immobilization, catalysis, and electrochemical engineering.
method: Enumerate mineral surface terminations and build bulk reference cells for surface-energy evaluation.
Use DFT labels to train a machine-learning interatomic potential (MinNEP).
Combine predicted surface energies with Wulff construction to identify stable terminations and crystal morphologies.
evidence_signal: Across ten minerals, MinSurfSet includes 764 surface slabs and 90 oriented unit cells; MinNEP reaches 0.0119 eV/Å² mean absolute error and about 1.14×10^4 acceleration versus DFT.
watch_item: Results still depend on the quality and coverage of the enumerated training surfaces, so rare terminations outside the sampled space may remain less reliable.
paper_title: MinSurf: resolving the atomic-scale stability landscape of mineral surfaces
